{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "59e8b9fe",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Comparing several cavities\n",
    "\n",
    "A `Study` holds many cavities and runs an analysis across all of them, so the result namespaces offer **comparison plots** — every cavity's figures of merit side by side. Here we sweep the iris radius of a single-cell elliptical cavity and compare how the fundamental-mode QOIs respond."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "73503981",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-19T20:54:04.476465Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-19T20:54:04.476328Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.259231Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.258463Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from cavsim2d import Study, EllipticalCavity\n",
    "from cavsim2d.utils.style import apply_style\n",
    "apply_style()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b821c3b0",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. A study of three iris radii\n",
    "\n",
    "The mid-cell template is the TESLA cell; we vary only the iris radius `Ri` (index 4 of `[A, B, a, b, Ri, L, Req]`)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "9f3a0bc6",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.261593Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.261333Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.288876Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.287882Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "base = [42, 42, 12, 19, 35, 57.652, 103.3536]\n",
    "study = Study('compare_demo')\n",
    "cavs, names = [], []\n",
    "for Ri in (30, 35, 40):\n",
    "    cell = base.copy(); cell[4] = Ri\n",
    "    cavs.append(EllipticalCavity(1, cell, cell, cell, beampipe='both',\n",
    "                                 name=f'Ri{Ri}'))\n",
    "    names.append(f'Ri = {Ri} mm')\n",
    "study.add_cavity(cavs, names)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "42a055c1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Solve them all\n",
    "\n",
    "One call runs the eigenmode analysis for every cavity in the study."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "07fe8629",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.291619Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-19T20:54:06.291366Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-19T20:54:17.395176Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-19T20:54:17.394441Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "study.run_eigenmode({'processes': 1, 'boundary_conditions': 'mm',\n",
    "                     'mesh_config': {'h': 10, 'p': 2}})"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "694bc4cb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Compare the figures of merit\n",
    "\n",
    "`study.eigenmode.plot_fm_scatter()` overlays each cavity's fundamental-mode QOIs. A wider iris lowers the frequency and R/Q and raises the peak-field ratios — the classic aperture trade-off."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "b41210b9",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-19T20:54:17.397791Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-19T20:54:17.397561Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-19T20:54:18.206043Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-19T20:54:18.205028Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x300 with 4 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "{'freq [MHz]': <Axes: label='freq [MHz]', ylabel='$f$ [MHz]'>,\n",
       " 'R/Q [Ohm]': <Axes: label='R/Q [Ohm]', ylabel='$R/Q ~\\\\mathrm{[\\\\Omega]}$'>,\n",
       " 'Epk/Eacc []': <Axes: label='Epk/Eacc []', ylabel='$E_\\\\mathrm{pk}/E_\\\\mathrm{acc} ~[\\\\cdot]$'>,\n",
       " 'Bpk/Eacc [mT/MV/m]': <Axes: label='Bpk/Eacc [mT/MV/m]', ylabel='$B_\\\\mathrm{pk}/E_\\\\mathrm{acc} ~\\\\mathrm{[mT/MV/m]}$'>}"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 4,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "study.eigenmode.plot_fm_scatter(qois=['freq [MHz]', 'R/Q [Ohm]',\n",
    "                                      'Epk/Eacc []', 'Bpk/Eacc [mT/MV/m]'])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a3a5d1bb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. The same data as bars\n"
   ]
  },

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "6f2e71c8",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Where to go next\n",
    "\n",
    "- `study.eigenmode.plot_fm_scatter(uq=True)` adds ±1σ error bars when the cavities were run with a `uq_config` — see [Eigenmode under uncertainty](../advanced/eigenmode_uq.ipynb).\n",
    "- `study.wakefield.plot_hom_scatter(...)` and `Study.plot_all_scatter(...)` compare wakefield and cross-domain QOIs the same way."
   ]
  }
